title: My way to school or work
summary: Describe your daily route to school or work.
topic: My way to school or work
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about your route to school or work. Describe how you travel, what you pass on the way, and how long it takes. Use present simple and sequence words.
hintStarters: Every morning, I leave home at…
I take the … to get to…

hintVocab.meaningVi: hành trình đi làm/học hằng ngày

sampleText: Every morning, I leave my apartment at seven thirty to go to work. I work at a design company in the centre of Mexico City. First, I walk five minutes to the subway station on my street. The station is always crowded at rush hour. I take the metro for about twenty minutes. I always stand near the door because I get off at a busy stop. Then I transfer to another line and I ride for five more minutes. After that, I walk from the exit to my office. The walk takes about ten minutes and I pass a market and a small park. I like this part of the commute because the park has big trees and the air feels fresh. I arrive at the office at about eight thirty. Some days the subway is very slow and I am late. On those days, I listen to music and I try not to feel stressed. The whole journey is about forty minutes.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: leave my apartment at seven thirty
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: rời căn hộ lúc bảy rưỡi
đông đúc vào giờ cao điểm

sampleAnnotations.phrase: get off at a busy stop
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: xuống ở một trạm đông người

sampleAnnotations.phrase: walk from the exit to my office
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đi bộ từ lối ra đến văn phòng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a market and a small park
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một khu chợ và một công viên nhỏ
không khí cảm thấy trong lành
cố không cảm thấy căng thẳng

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for daily routines
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì hiện tại đơn cho những việc xảy ra đều đặn mỗi ngày: 'I leave', 'I take', 'I transfer', 'I arrive'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple for things that happen every day: 'I leave at seven thirty', 'I take the metro for about twenty minutes'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Prepositions of time and duration: at, for, about
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'at' + giờ cụ thể: 'at seven thirty'; 'for' + khoảng thời gian: 'for twenty minutes'; 'about' = khoảng chừng.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'at' + a specific time ('at seven thirty'), 'for' + a duration ('for twenty minutes'), 'about' means approximately.

sampleTranslationVi: Mỗi sáng, tôi rời căn hộ lúc bảy rưỡi để đi làm. Tôi làm việc tại một công ty thiết kế ở trung tâm thành phố Mexico. Đầu tiên, tôi đi bộ năm phút đến ga tàu điện ngầm trên phố tôi ở. Ga luôn đông đúc vào giờ cao điểm. Tôi đi tàu khoảng hai mươi phút. Tôi luôn đứng gần cửa vì tôi xuống ở một trạm đông người. Sau đó tôi chuyển sang tuyến khác và đi thêm năm phút. Tiếp theo, tôi đi bộ từ lối ra đến văn phòng. Đoạn đi bộ mất khoảng mười phút và tôi đi qua một khu chợ và một công viên nhỏ. Tôi thích phần hành trình này vì công viên có những cây lớn và không khí cảm thấy trong lành. Tôi đến văn phòng khoảng tám rưỡi. Có những ngày tàu rất chậm và tôi bị muộn. Những ngày đó, tôi nghe nhạc và cố không cảm thấy căng thẳng. Toàn bộ hành trình mất khoảng bốn mươi phút.

mcQuestions.prompt: What time does the writer leave home?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I leave my apartment at seven thirty'.

mcQuestions.prompt: What type of transport does the writer mainly use?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I take the metro for about twenty minutes'.

mcQuestions.prompt: How long does the walk from the exit to the office take?
What does the writer pass on the way to the office?
options: A market and a small park.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I pass a market and a small park'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Why does the writer like the park part of the commute?
options: Because they can sit on a bench.
Because it has big trees and fresh air.
Because there is a café there.
Because it is faster than the metro.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'the park has big trees and the air feels fresh'.
mcQuestions.hint: Two things are mentioned about the park.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the writer do on days when the subway is slow?
options: They listen to music and try not to feel stressed.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I listen to music and I try not to feel stressed'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for what happens on bad commute days.

mcQuestions.prompt: How long does the total journey take?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'The whole journey is about forty minutes'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means very full of people?
mcQuestions.explanation: 'crowded' means too many people in one place.
mcQuestions.hint: Rush hour is connected to this word.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'for' to describe duration correctly?
options: I take the metro at twenty minutes.
I take the metro for twenty minutes.
I take the metro in twenty minutes.
I take the metro since twenty minutes.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'for' is used with a duration: 'for twenty minutes'.
mcQuestions.hint: Which preposition goes with a length of time?

mcQuestions.prompt: What does 'transfer' mean on public transport?
options: Change from one line or vehicle to another.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'transfer' means to change from one train or bus line to another.
mcQuestions.hint: You do this when your journey needs more than one line.

options: At a school in the suburbs.
At a design company in the centre of Mexico City.
At a market near the park.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I work at a design company in the centre of Mexico City'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: What are some ways people make a long or boring commute more enjoyable?
